title: Chiliadenus hesperius
taxonomy: Chiliadenus hesperius is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants. The genus Chiliadenus is part of the tribe Gnaphalieae, a group of small to medium-sized flowering plants. The specific classification of Chiliadenus hesperius is further defined by its unique characteristics and growth habits. It is native to regions with dry and temperate climates, such as the Mediterranean and parts of North Africa.

intro: Chiliadenus hesperius, also known as the Greek daphne, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate congestion and promote healing. Bioactive constituents of Chiliadenus hesperius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
